title: Link admin accounts to player records via user.player_id
description: The captain dashboard needs to know which player a logged-in admin is, so it can default 'My Teams' scope correctly and personalise the UI. Add a one-time self-link flow where an admin on first visit to the dashboard picks their player record from the club roster.
acceptance_criteria: On first visit to /admin/league/planning after this WI lands, an admin is presented with an 'I am…' picker to link their user to a player
Picker shows active players at the home club, searchable by name
Selection writes users.player_id for that user
Once linked, the picker does not reappear — the dashboard uses the linked player for personalisation
Admin can unlink / relink from a small settings affordance on the dashboard (in case of mistake)
The link is optional — admins without a linked player still see the full dashboard but default to 'All Teams' scope
Admin users with a linked player who is also in the captains table see their team(s) highlighted subtly, but are never restricted from any scope

files_to_modify: internal/models/models.go — User.PlayerID field already added in Sprint 016 WI-094
design_notes: No hard-coding of which admins are captains — captain status derived from the captains table at query time
All captains see all teams per the team philosophy — the link is for personalisation only, never for gating

testing_requirements: Integration test: admin first visit shows picker; after selection, subsequent visits don't

context.user_story: As a captain, I want the planning dashboard to know I'm James so it can default to showing my teams first — but I don't want to be locked out of seeing any other team.
context.team_philosophy: We're a whole team. Any captain can pick for any team (covering holidays, joint decisions). Scope filters are for focus, never isolation.
